question: Identify a specific California county seat based on a set of health and housing statistics. I'm looking for a county that had an Area Median Income of at least $142,800 in 2022 and $147,900 in 2023. In terms of assistance costs, this county should have had a women, infants and children average cost per family of $570 or more in 2018 and $700 or more in 2020. It also needs to show a 2015 perioperative hemorrhage or hematoma observed rate of 19 or higher and have a geographical shape area of at least 3 billion square meters. Once you identify the county that matches all these figures, could you tell me which ocean the bay on the southern shore of its county seat connects to? Only report that ocean. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
practical_motivation: A policy analyst studying how high-income counties still experience elevated WIC redemption costs and hospital safety events wants to pinpoint the county that best fits those criteria, then relate its county seat to the region's coastal geography for a public briefing.

1.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/income-limits-by-county/files/2022-income-limits.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

df["AMI"] = pd.to_numeric(df["AMI"], errors="coerce")
result = df.loc[df["AMI"] >= 142800, ["County", "AMI"]].copy()
result = result.sort_values(["AMI", "County"], ascending=[False, True])
answer = {row["County"]: int(row["AMI"]) for _, row in result.iterrows()}
1.subquestion: Which counties have AMI >= 142800 in the 2022 income limits data? (Filter: AMI >= 142800; list County and AMI.)

3.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/california-women-infants-and-children-program-redemption-by-county-967e9/files/2010-2018-part-a-wic-redemptions-by-vendor-county-with-family-counts.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

counties = ["Santa Clara", "Marin", "San Francisco", "San Mateo", "Alameda", "Contra Costa"]
df["Obligation Year and Month"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Obligation Year and Month"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df.loc[
    df["Obligation Year and Month"].eq(2018) & df["Vendor County"].isin(counties),
    ["Vendor County", "Average Cost per Family"],
].copy()
filtered["Average Cost per Family"] = pd.to_numeric(
    filtered["Average Cost per Family"].astype(str).str.replace(r"[^0-9.\-]", "", regex=True),
    errors="coerce",
)
answer = (
    filtered.loc[filtered["Average Cost per Family"] >= 570, "Vendor County"]
    .dropna()
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values()
    .tolist()
)
3.subquestion: Among the counties from hop 1, which have Average Cost per Family >= $570 in the 2018 WIC redemptions data? (Filter: Obligation Year and Month == 2018; Vendor County in <hop 1>; parse Average Cost per Family; keep >= 570.)

5.fact:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/hospitalization-counts-and-rates-of-selected-adverse-hospital-events-by-california-county-67a4a/files/ca-oshpd-adveventhospitalizationspsi-county2005-2015q3.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

counties = ["Alameda", "San Francisco", "Santa Clara"]
df["Year"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Year"], errors="coerce")
df["PSI"] = pd.to_numeric(df["PSI"], errors="coerce")
df["ObsRate"] = pd.to_numeric(df["ObsRate"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df.loc[
    df["Year"].eq(2015) & df["PSI"].eq(27) & df["County"].isin(counties),
    ["County", "ObsRate"],
].copy()
answer = (
    filtered.loc[filtered["ObsRate"] >= 19, "County"]
    .dropna()
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values()
    .tolist()
)
5.subquestion: Among the counties from hop 2, which have ObsRate >= 19 for PSI 27 (Perioperative Hemorrhage or Hematoma) in 2015? (Filter: Year == 2015; PSI == 27; County in <hop 2>; read ObsRate; keep >= 19.)

import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/california-county-boundaries-bd0f6/files/California_County_Boundaries_9101614255844818638.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

counties = ["Alameda", "San Francisco", "Santa Clara"]
df["Shape__Area"] = pd.to_numeric(df["Shape__Area"], errors="coerce")
filtered = df.loc[
    df["COUNTY_NAME"].isin(counties),
    ["COUNTY_NAME", "Shape__Area"],
].copy()
answer = (
    filtered.loc[filtered["Shape__Area"] >= 3000000000, "COUNTY_NAME"]
    .dropna()
    .drop_duplicates()
    .sort_values()
    .tolist()
)
6.subquestion: Among the counties from hop 2, which have Shape__Area >= 3000000000 in the California county boundaries data? (Filter: COUNTY_NAME in <hop 2>; read Shape__Area; keep >= 3000000000.)

7.fact: The county seat and largest city of Santa Clara County is San Jose.
7.subquestion: What is the county seat of the county identified in hop 3?

8.fact: San Jose is located on the southern shore of San Francisco Bay.
8.subquestion: On which bay's southern shore is the county seat from hop 4 located?

9.fact: San Francisco Bay connects to the Pacific Ocean via the Golden Gate strait.
9.subquestion: Which ocean does the bay from hop 5 connect to via the Golden Gate strait?

Node 8: San Jose on southern shore of -> San Francisco Bay
